original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.version: NICE guideline NG198, last updated 30 April 2026
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: People with acne vulgaris, including people with acne-related scarring.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist acne assessment, follow-up, information, and clinician-led management discussion.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting acne-scar concerns, ongoing acne, change over time, psychological impact, and clinician review. It does not grade scars automatically, recommend a procedure, medicine, or referral, or infer eligibility.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.2 — acne-related scarring concerns and specialist review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting scar concerns, possible ongoing acne, time since acne cleared, change over time, psychological distress, severity already assessed by a clinician, and completed specialist discussion without generating treatment or referral.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.7.5 — maintenance and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording ongoing acne or relapse context and a clinician-completed maintenance review; no treatment is generated.

title: Acne vulgaris: management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: People with acne vulgaris, including people with acne-related scarring.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist acne assessment, follow-up, information, and clinician-led management discussion.
